key_insight: A self-supervised mask-aware Transformer can learn force estimation that remains accurate even when FBG sensor channels are partially missing or fractured.
engineering_relevance: This provides a scalable alternative to per-failure model banks for catheter-scale surgical robotics, improving robustness without requiring exhaustive calibration for every dropout pattern.
method: Pretrain a Transformer encoder with masked-channel reconstruction on unlabeled sensor streams.
Fine-tune with a balanced clean-versus-corrupted objective and a dynamic corruption curriculum.
Add a heteroscedastic uncertainty head to predict per-axis confidence in one forward pass.
evidence_signal: On an 8-channel FBG dataset, the model achieved 0.0066 N RMSE in nominal conditions and 0.0126 N with severe 4-channel failures, outperforming a 255-model bank at 0.0154 N under the same loss.
watch_item: Performance depends on representative corruption patterns during pretraining and fine-tuning, so rare real-world failure modes may still require additional validation.
paper_title: Self-Supervised Mask-Aware Transformers for Fault-Tolerant FBG Force Sensing in Minimally Invasive Surgical Robotics
